reason: All four residual findings are minor. The section merges are content-complete and defensible. The missing hints reduce routing precision but do not eliminate user access to the information — each missing class name already appears in hints for another section that holds the primary content for that class. No finding rises to medium or high impact.

findings_assessment.justification: Both merges (s2 combines two closely-related env-config headings; s7 combines init + retrieval steps) are content-complete and well under the 2000-char threshold. All source content is present in the knowledge file. A user querying this topic will receive correct, complete answers regardless of the section count discrepancy. Phase E does not restructure sections — it is scoped to content and hint fixes — so this finding was never a candidate for automated repair.

XmlComponentDefinitionLoader appears in s2 only inside a warning note explaining that bootstrap frameworks handle it — it is not the primary subject of s2. A user querying by this class name would be routed to s5 (which already lists XmlComponentDefinitionLoader in hints) and would receive the correct conceptual content. The absence from s2 hints means only the bootstrap-warning context is harder to reach by class-name query, which is a minor precision loss. Phase E output is byte-for-byte identical to Phase D input for s2 hints, indicating the fix agent failed to write the change.

XmlComponentDefinitionLoader is actually present in the s6 hints array in both the final knowledge file and the Phase E output — Phase D's finding was incorrect on that point. The only genuinely missing hint is HelloComponent. HelloComponent's class definition and primary description live in s4 (which already carries HelloComponent in hints), so a class-name query would land on s4. s6 shows it used in a getComponentByName call, which is illustrative but not the authoritative source for HelloComponent. Impact is low routing-precision loss only. Phase E reproduced the existing s6 hints unchanged, confirming the fix was not applied.

XmlComponentDefinitionLoader is already covered by s5 and s6 hints; HelloComponent and HelloMessageProvider are covered by s4 hints. The primary query patterns for s7 content — 'how to initialize SystemRepository with DiContainer', 'ObjectLoader usage' — are served by the existing hints (SystemRepository初期化, ObjectLoader, DiContainer, getObject). The missing class names would only affect queries that use a specific class name as a proxy for the SystemRepository-initialization topic, and those users would still receive closely related content from s4/s5/s6. Phase E output is identical to Phase D input for s7 hints — fixes were not applied by the fix agent.
